Purpose: Performs all steps for model-checking and parameters estimation for ABC analyses. abcrf includes random forest methods (a type of supervised machine-learning)
Specifics: Informative vignette, allows graphical representation, complete and robust
Issues and warnings: Does not perform coalescent simulations (but can be used in combination with coala)

Purpose: Fitting demographic histories
Specifics: Fast, Automated. Provides a user-friendly interface as a singularity container. A particular strength is its ability to model linked selection and barriers to gene flow. Similar to DIYABC, all diagnostics and simulations are performed automatically.
Issues and warnings: The method assumes no linkage between loci. Not suited for recent divergence. Not suited for more than 2 populations. Infers the most likely demographic scenario before estimating the effects of linked selection

Purpose: Fitting demographic histories and identify genomic islands
Specifics: Fast, Automated through SnakeMake and a singularity container. Unlike DILS, can work on successive, linked genomic windows. Like DILS, can model linked selection and barriers to gene flow. Similar to DIYAB, all diagnostics and simulations are performed automatically.
Issues and warnings: Not suited for recent divergence. Not suited for more than 2 populations. Can use a recombination map. I would recommend using a VCF with invariant sites to limit bias in summary statistics estimation. Infers the most likely demographic scenario before estimating the effects of linked selection

Purpose: Fitting demographic histories and identify genomic islands
Specifics: First models an Isolation-with-Migration model, then identifies variation in migration and Ne along slidiing windows in the genome. Can take VCF and BAM files as input.
Issues and warnings: Not suited for recent divergence. Not suited for more than 2 populations. Does not model interruptions in gene flow (continuous IM model). Available through conda (be careful with dependencies).

Purpose: Complete ABC analysis, from simulations to model checking and parameters estimation
Specifics: Modular, facilitates the computation of summary statistics
Issues and warnings: NA

Purpose: Complete ABC analysis, from simulations to model checking and parameters estimation
Specifics: User-friendly. Many ways to check goodness-of-fit. Good introduction to ABC models.
Issues and warnings: Does not model continuous gene flow.

Purpose: Inferring change in Ne using whole-genome data
Specifics: Supposed to better assess recent events. Uses a set of summary statistics for the AFS and LD between markers. Handles multiple individuals
Issues and warnings: Approximate bayesian approaches do not retrieve the whole information

Purpose: Combining coalescent simulators within a single framework
Specifics: Facilitates the building of scenarios and computes summary statistics for simulations. Can be easily combined with the abc or abc-rf packages in R.
Issues and warnings: Includes so far ms, msms and scrm

Purpose: Simulate demographic scenarios for asexual/facultatively sexual species
Specifics: Can handle varying levels of sexual reproduction, inbreeding, selfing and cloning.
Issues and warnings: Does not handle population size changes nor selection yet.

Purpose: Building any arbitrary scenario using a coalescent framework
Specifics: Any arbitrary scenario can be implemented. Handles SNP, microsatellites and sequence data.
Issues and warnings: Does not handle selection. Slower than ms with no recombination, much faster with recombination (see manual)

Purpose: Building any arbitrary scenario using a coalescent framework
Specifics: Any arbitrary scenario can be implemented. Handles SNP, microsatellites and sequence data. msms can include selection in the model.
Issues and warnings: Syntax can be difficult to handle for new users compared to, e.g., fastsimcoal2 (but see coala)
Reference: (Hudson, 2002; Ewing and Hermisson, 2010; Pavlidis et al., 2010)

Purpose: Simulate demographic scenarios including selection
Specifics: Flexible, syntax similar to ms, handles arbitratily complex models. Can be used in an ABC framework to include selection as a parameter to be estimated
Issues and warnings: Syntax can be difficult to handle for the naive user (but see coala)

Purpose: Fast simulation of chromosome-scale sequences
Specifics: Syntax similar to ms, handles any arbitrary scenario
Issues and warnings: Does not handle gene conversion and fixed number of segregating sites (unlike ms)

Purpose: Simulating demographic scenarios in their spatial context
Specifics: Coalescent simulator for genetic data, forward-in-time for demography in space. Spatially explicit.
Issues and warnings: Simulations can be slow (>1 hour) for large datasets (>100,000 SNPs) over more than 1,000 generations. Does not incorporate selection

Purpose: Simulate selective sweeps under arbitrary demographic scenarios
Specifics: Relatively fast for short genomic fragments. Designed to simulate "hard" and "soft" sweeps.
Issues and warnings: Mostly used with diploS/HIC. Other simulators such as msms may be more suited for some scenarios.

Purpose: Simulating demographic and selection scenarios in their spatial context
Specifics: Comprehensive simulator.Designed for the study of selection in a spatially-explicit context. Simulates quantitative traits, fitness landscapes and underlying genetic variation with migration. Include both population and individual-based simulations
Issues and warnings: Scan be slow for large/complex models

Purpose: Simulating genomic sequences with intrinsic and extrinsic factors
Specifics: One of the most comprehensive simulators. Can simulate genetic data in their spatio-temporal context, the effects of selection at linked sites, coding and non-coding variation, inbreeding and selfing. Supports tree-sequence recording for faster simulations. Large community.
Issues and warnings: Slow for large genomic regions/large populations

Purpose: Detecting selective sweeps
Specifics: Classifies genomic windows as neutral, selected, or impacted by selection at linked sites. Also distinguishes between selection on standing and de novo variation. Uses a set of summary statistics describing frequency spectrum and LD, does not require phasing. Good tutorial explaining the pipeline.
Issues and warnings: Good performance depends on the parameters used to simulate sweeps (window size, selective coefficient, demography). Requires some trial and error for new model species. Interpretation of "soft" and "hard" sweeps remains discussed.

Purpose: Detecting selective sweeps, balancing selection, and estimate demographic history
Specifics: Uses deep-learning algorithms to classify genomic regions as selected or neutral, and estimate effective population sizes. Flexible (any number of summary statistics can be provided by the investigator).
Issues and warnings: Requires summary statistics as an input. Difficult for a naïve user.

Purpose: Estimating effective recombination rates
Specifics: Uses regression to estimate effective recombination rates from SNP alignments. Can use the VCF format. No clear bias due to phasing errors observed. Can incorporate demographic history (using ms command line).
Issues and warnings: Requires phased data.

Purpose: Detecting introgression
Specifics: Uses Extra Trees classifiers and dedicated summary statistics to classify genomic windows as being introgressed or not. Identifies the direction of introgression.
Issues and warnings: Targets pulse of introgression rather than continuous gene flow, but can detect the latter. Requires phased data in a fasta format.

Purpose: Detecting adaptive introgression
Specifics: Uses convolutional neural networks to identify adaptive introgression. Trained using the tree-sequence records obtained from SliM3. Can handle VCF files and unphased data.
Issues and warnings: Strong computational bottleneck with SliM simulations.

Purpose: Detecting selective sweeps
Specifics: Uses convolutional neural networks to classify genomic windows in bins of distinct selection coefficients. Directly uses the image of the alignment, avoiding compression (i.e. using summary statistics)
Issues and warnings: Can be slow for large datasets.

Purpose: Estimating recombination rates
Specifics: Uses recurrent neural networks to estimate recombination rates from SNP alignments. Handles unphased and pooled data. Uses msprime (Python implementation of ms) to generate simulations upon which the algorithm is trained. Can incorporate known demographic history provided by the user.
Issues and warnings: Can be computationnally intensive for large effective population sizes. Accuracy on pooled data is modest for low depth of coverage. Absolute estimates of recombination rates depends on the accuracy of the mutation rate used for simulations.

Purpose: Detecting selective sweeps
Specifics: Uses averaged one-dependence estimator to classify genomic regions as selected or neutral. Flexible in terms of which summary statistics are used. Can incorporate demographic history.
Issues and warnings: Requires summary statistics as an input. Only distinguishes between selective sweeps and neutral regions.

Purpose: Coalescent / hybrid forward-backward simulation of large samples and complex demographies
Specifics: v1.0 adds a discrete-time Wright-Fisher (DTWF) regime, finite-site mutations, gene conversion, multiple-merger coalescents and tree-sequence output. Native consumer of demes models.
Issues and warnings: DTWF is much slower than Hudson coalescent; pick regime carefully. Mutation models more flexible than older versions.

Purpose: Forward Wright-Fisher and non-WF simulation; nucleotide- and tree-sequence-aware; supports continuous space, multispecies and arbitrary fitness functions
Specifics: Major release adds multispecies models, improved performance, and tighter tskit/pyslim integration. Now the de facto standard for forward simulation in popgen.
Issues and warnings: Heavier than coalescent simulators; scaling tricks (Q-rescaling) often required for large Ne.

Purpose: Reproducible demographic + DFE models across many species, callable as msprime or SLiM simulations
Specifics: Catalog of curated demographic models, recombination maps and distributions of fitness effects (DFE catalog 2023). Crucial for benchmarking ML methods.
Issues and warnings: Catalog completeness varies by clade; some species have only minimal models.

Class of method: Spatial / R interface for SLiM and msprime
Purpose: Spatially explicit popgen simulation in R; supports both panmictic and continuous-space models
Specifics: R DSL that compiles to SLiM and msprime; tree-sequence native; integrates with admixr and demogr.
Issues and warnings: Spatial models rely on the SLiM backend (slower).

Purpose: Python interface to fwdpp for forward-time simulation with custom Python fitness models
Specifics: Tree-sequence recording compatible with tskit; flexible for non-standard selection scenarios.
Issues and warnings: Less feature-rich than SLiM but easier to embed in Python pipelines.

Purpose: Post-process SLiM tree-sequence output (recapitate, overlay neutral mutations, simplify)
Specifics: Critical companion of SLiM for hybrid forward-backward simulation.
Issues and warnings: Recapitation requires care with population structure and ancestry.

Purpose: Coalescent simulation of selective sweeps under various models (hard, soft, partial, recurrent)
Specifics: Frequently used for training data of ML sweep classifiers (diploS/HIC, Flex-sweep).
Issues and warnings: Stand-alone executable; less integrated than msprime / SLiM.

Purpose: Tidy R framework for simulation-based ABC inference of demographic models, integrated with slendr
Specifics: Lowers the barrier for running custom ABC pipelines on tree sequences.
Issues and warnings: Newer tool; benchmarking is ongoing.

Purpose: Train CNNs on simulated data for demographic inference
Specifics: Generic deep-learning framework for popgen; outputs uncertainty estimates and supports model selection.
Issues and warnings: Out-of-distribution failure when real data deviate from training simulations.

Purpose: Demographic inference using neural networks trained on dadi/moments simulations
Specifics: Bridge between simulation-based ML and SFS-based inference; useful for large parameter spaces.
Issues and warnings: Reproducibility depends on the simulation pipeline; benchmarks against dadi/moments are recommended.

Purpose: Generic frameworks for sweep / introgression classification trained on simulations
Specifics: Includes diploS/HIC, partialS/HIC, Flex-sweep, IntroUNET, MaLAdapt, ERICA. Listed here to highlight the simulation-training workflow. See other tables for more details.
Issues and warnings: Out-of-distribution misclassification is the dominant failure mode; require simulation-based calibration with stdpopsim.

Purpose: Python library for VCF/zarr-based summary statistics, useful as input to ML pipelines
Specifics: Now widely used as a feature-extractor for simulation-based inference.
Issues and warnings: Not an inference method itself; user must validate statistics.

Purpose: Discriminator-based deep-learning demographic inference
Specifics: Uses GAN-style discriminators to compare simulated vs. real data; integrates with stdpopsim.
Issues and warnings: Newer tool; demographic bias from training simulations possible.

Purpose: Simulate sweeps and structured-coalescent backgrounds in msprime
Specifics: msprime 1.x can simulate selective sweeps via discrete-time machinery and has growing support for selection.
Issues and warnings: Selection support is more limited than in SLiM; complex DFEs need forward simulation.

Purpose: Custom ABC / likelihood-free inference using SLiM as the simulator
Specifics: Workflows exist for inferring DFEs, dominance, and complex demography on top of SLiM 4.
Issues and warnings: Heavy compute; requires careful summary-statistic design.
